page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 56 vCPUs mapped to 28 physical cores with multithreading enabled, and provides 448 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The x86_64 architecture supports instruction sets including AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier, and small-block cached read bandwidth also ranking in the top 25%. However, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths fall into the average category.

Computational workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing perform in the top 25% tier. For database and memory-bound operations, the instance achieves strong results, including top 25% performance in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations. Large language model inference speeds for a small 135M model reach the top 10% tier for text generation, while medium 7B model text generation remains in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per vCPU provides a balanced resource profile for memory-intensive applications without the overhead of dedicated storage or GPU hardware. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 56 vCPUs and 448 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 28 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers strong multi-core CPU and database performance, placing in the top 25% for Passmark CPU and Redis SET operations, while its memory performance ranks in the top 10% tier. Tradeoffs include a reliance on network-attached storage due to the absence of local disks. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is a memory-optimized server with 56 vCPUs, 448 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU. It offers top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 features 56 vCPUs, 448 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense server featuring fifty-six virtual cores and top-tier memory performance for database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 56 vCPUs and 448 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Top 10% tier Passmark memory score and strong small-block read bandwidth
Top 25% performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and software compilation
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Suited for memory-intensive databases, compilation, and CPU-based inference
